  ---
  PHASE 4 — AUTONOMY & CONTROL

  Tier 1: Fully Autonomous (No Confirmation)

  - Reading any data (email, calendar, files, web)
  - Searching and summarizing information
  - Taking notes, saving to knowledge base
  - Sending notifications to the user
  - Running scheduled briefings
  - Monitoring tasks (price alerts, page changes)
  - Drafting content (saved as draft, not sent)
  - File organization within designated folders
  - Responding to messages the user has pre-approved templates for

  Tier 2: Notify Before Action (3-second window to cancel)

  - Sending emails to known contacts about routine matters
  - Creating calendar events
  - Moving/archiving emails
  - Running shell commands that don't modify system state
  - Posting to Slack in low-stakes channels

  Tier 3: Requires Explicit Approval

  - Sending emails to new contacts or about sensitive topics
  - Deleting anything (files, emails, events)
  - Financial actions (purchases, transfers)
  - Posting publicly (social media, GitHub issues)
  - Modifying system configuration
  - Running destructive shell commands
  - Sharing data with external services
  - Any action involving credentials or authentication

  Safety Mechanisms

  Action Classifier — every action gets scored on:
  {
    "reversibility": 0.0-1.0,    // can this be undone?
    "visibility": 0.0-1.0,       // does this affect others?
    "financial_impact": 0.0-1.0,  // does this cost money?
    "data_sensitivity": 0.0-1.0   // does this touch sensitive data?
  }
  Composite score determines tier. Threshold adjustable per user.

  Dry Run Mode — for any Tier 2/3 action, system can simulate first:
  "I would send this email to jane@acme.com with subject 'Q3 Report'.
   Approve / Edit / Cancel?"

  Undo Log — every mutating action logged with enough state to reverse:
  {
    "action": "archive_email",
    "target": "msg_abc123",
    "previous_state": {"labels": ["INBOX"]},
    "reverse_action": "move_to_inbox",
    "timestamp": "2026-04-12T14:30:00Z",
    "ttl": "72h"
  }

  Error Handling
  - Retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts) for transient failures (network,
  rate limits)
  - Degrade gracefully: if one tool fails in a chain, complete what's possible,
  report what failed
  - Never retry destructive actions automatically
  - On unexpected state: halt, snapshot context, notify user
  - Circuit breaker: if a tool fails >5 times in an hour, disable it and alert

  ---
  PHASE 5 — MEMORY SYSTEM

  Layer 1: Session Context (In-Context Window)

  - Current conversation turns
  - Active task state and plan
  - Recently accessed files/data
  - Implementation: LLM context window, managed by sliding window + summarization
  when approaching limit

  Layer 2: Working Memory (Redis)

  - Currently executing task graphs
  - Pending approvals queue
  - Active monitoring jobs and their state
  - Conversation summaries from last 24h
  - TTL: 7 days, then archived or promoted

  Layer 3: Long-Term Semantic Memory (Vector DB — Qdrant)

  Collection: conversations
    - Chunked conversation turns, embedded
    - Metadata: date, topic, participants, sentiment

  Collection: documents
    - Ingested PDFs, emails, notes, web pages
    - Metadata: source, date, author, tags

  Collection: decisions
    - Past decisions user made and reasoning
    - Metadata: domain, outcome (if known), confidence
  - Retrieval: hybrid search (semantic + keyword + metadata filter)
  - Embedding model: text-embedding-3-large or local nomic-embed

  Layer 4: Structured Knowledge (Postgres)

  -- People the user interacts with
  CREATE TABLE contacts (
    id UUID, name TEXT, relationship TEXT,
    organization TEXT, communication_style TEXT,
    last_interaction TIMESTAMP, notes TEXT
  );

  -- User preferences (learned over time)
  CREATE TABLE preferences (
    domain TEXT,        -- "email", "scheduling", "food", "travel"
    key TEXT,           -- "preferred_airline", "meeting_length", "email_tone"
    value JSONB,
    confidence FLOAT,   -- how sure we are about this preference
    learned_from TEXT,  -- which interaction taught us this
    updated_at TIMESTAMP
  );

  -- Task history
  CREATE TABLE task_log (
    id UUID, description TEXT, plan JSONB,
    result TEXT, status TEXT,
    user_feedback TEXT,  -- "good", "wrong", "partially correct"
    created_at TIMESTAMP
  );

  Learning Loop

  User interaction
    → Was the outcome approved/rejected/modified?
      → If approved: increase confidence in similar future actions
      → If modified: store the correction as a preference
      → If rejected: store as negative example, lower confidence threshold
    → Weekly: analyze task_log for patterns
      → Extract new preferences
      → Identify recurring tasks → suggest automation
      → Prune low-confidence preferences that were never reinforced

  ---
  PHASE 6 — WORKFLOW ENGINE

  Workflow Definition (DAG-based)

  # Example: Daily Email Triage
  name: email_triage
  trigger: cron("0 8 * * *")  # 8 AM daily
  steps:
    - id: fetch
      action: email.fetch_unread
      params: { max: 50 }

    - id: classify
      action: llm.classify
      input: $fetch.results
      params:
        categories:
          - urgent_action_needed
          - needs_reply_today
          - informational
          - spam_or_irrelevant
      parallel: true  # classify all emails in parallel

    - id: summarize_urgent
      action: llm.summarize
      input: $classify.results[category=urgent_action_needed]
      depends_on: classify

    - id: draft_replies
      action: llm.draft_email_reply
      input: $classify.results[category=needs_reply_today]
      depends_on: classify
      approval: tier2  # user sees drafts before sending

    - id: archive_spam
      action: email.archive
      input: $classify.results[category=spam_or_irrelevant]
      depends_on: classify
      approval: tier1  # auto-execute

    - id: notify
      action: notify.push
      params:
        title: "Inbox Triage Complete"
        body: "$summarize_urgent.summary + ${len(draft_replies.results)} drafts
  ready"
      depends_on: [summarize_urgent, draft_replies, archive_spam]

  Workflow: Research → Summarize → Save → Notify

  trigger: user says "Research X"
    │
    ├─ Step 1: PLAN
    │   Planner breaks down:
    │   - What specific questions to answer
    │   - What sources to check
    │   - What format for output
    │
    ├─ Step 2: GATHER (parallel)
    │   ├─ Web search (3-5 queries, different angles)
    │   ├─ Scrape top 10 results
    │   ├─ Check knowledge base for existing notes on topic
    │   └─ Check email/docs for internal references
    │
    ├─ Step 3: SYNTHESIZE
    │   - LLM processes all gathered data
    │   - Structures into: key findings, sources, open questions
    │   - Verifier checks: are claims supported by sources?
    │
    ├─ Step 4: SAVE
    │   - Create note in knowledge base
    │   - Tag with topic, date, sources
    │   - Embed for future retrieval
    │
    └─ Step 5: DELIVER
        - Push notification with summary
        - Voice: "Research on X is ready. Key finding: ..."
        - Full report accessible in knowledge base

  Workflow: Monitor → Alert → Act

  trigger: user says "Watch competitor Y's pricing page"
    │
    ├─ SETUP
    │   - Store URL + baseline snapshot
    │   - Set check interval (default: 6h)
    │   - Define what constitutes a "change" (price fields, not footer)
    │
    ├─ MONITOR (recurring)
    │   - Fetch page → extract relevant content
    │   - Diff against baseline
    │   - If no meaningful change → sleep until next interval
    │
    ├─ ON CHANGE DETECTED
    │   ├─ Classify change severity (minor text edit vs price change)
    │   ├─ If significant:
    │   │   ├─ Screenshot before/after
    │   │   ├─ Summarize what changed
    │   │   ├─ Push notification immediately
    │   │   └─ Update knowledge base
    │   └─ If minor:
    │       └─ Log silently, include in next daily brief
    │
    └─ ERROR HANDLING
        - Page 404 → retry 3x over 1h → alert user "page may have moved"
        - Layout changed → re-identify target elements → if can't, ask user
        - Rate limited → back off, extend interval

  ---
  PHASE 7 — MULTI-AGENT DESIGN

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                  SUPERVISOR                       │
  │  Routes tasks, monitors agent health, enforces   │
  │  timeouts, handles escalation to human           │
  └──────┬────────┬────────┬────────┬───────────────┘
         │        │        │        │
    ┌────▼──┐ ┌──▼────┐ ┌─▼─────┐ ┌▼──────────┐
    │PLANNER│ │EXECUTOR│ │VERIFIER│ │  MEMORY   │
    │       │ │(pool)  │ │        │ │  MANAGER  │
    └───────┘ └────────┘ └────────┘ └───────────┘

  Planner Agent

  - Model: Opus (strongest reasoning)
  - Input: User request + relevant memories + current context
  - Output: Task DAG with dependencies, estimated durations, required tools
  - Behavior: Asks clarifying questions BEFORE planning if ambiguity > threshold.
  Considers user preferences from memory. Identifies risks and builds in
  checkpoints.

  Executor Agent(s)

  - Model: Sonnet (fast, capable, cost-effective) — multiple instances in parallel
  - Input: Single step from plan + required context
  - Output: Step result + status + any artifacts
  - Behavior: Executes exactly one step. Reports success/failure. Does not
  improvise beyond the step. If step fails, returns error context to Supervisor
  for replanning.

  Verifier Agent

  - Model: Sonnet with structured output
  - Input: Original request + plan + execution results
  - Output: {verified: bool, issues: [], suggestions: []}
  - Checks:
    - Did the output actually satisfy the request?
    - Are there factual errors? (cross-reference sources)
    - Were any steps skipped?
    - Is the output format correct?
    - Would the user likely accept this?

  Memory Manager

  - Model: Haiku (lightweight, runs frequently)
  - Runs: After every significant interaction
  - Tasks:
    - Extract facts, preferences, decisions from conversation
    - Update knowledge graph
    - Deduplicate and merge conflicting memories
    - Prune stale or low-confidence memories
    - Pre-fetch relevant memories for upcoming tasks (e.g., pull meeting notes
  before a scheduled call)

  Coordination Protocol

  1. User request → Supervisor
  2. Supervisor → Memory Manager: "fetch relevant context"
  3. Supervisor → Planner: request + context
  4. Planner returns DAG
  5. Supervisor validates DAG (safety check on all actions)
  6. Supervisor dispatches independent steps to Executor pool (parallel)
  7. As steps complete → unlock dependent steps → dispatch
  8. All steps done → Supervisor → Verifier
  9. Verifier passes → deliver to user
     Verifier fails → Supervisor → Planner: "replan step X, here's what went
  wrong"
  10. Supervisor → Memory Manager: "store interaction outcome"

  ---
  PHASE 8 — REALISTIC LIMITS (2026)

  Cannot Do Today

  ┌────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┐
  │       Limitation       │                            Why
       │
  ├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ True real-time screen  │ Vision models have ~1-2s latency. Can screenshot and
       │
  │ understanding          │ analyze, but can't "watch" a screen continuously at
       │
  │                        │ 30fps.
       │
  ├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Native app control     │ Can't click buttons in arbitrary desktop apps
  reliably.   │
  │ (non-API)              │ Accessibility APIs exist but are fragile. Browser
       │
  │                        │ automation is solid; desktop automation is not.
       │
  ├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Guaranteed correctness │ LLMs hallucinate. Every factual claim in a research
  task  │
  │                        │ must be treated as provisional.
       │
  ├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ True emotional         │ Can detect sentiment in text. Cannot genuinely
  understand │
  │ understanding          │  emotional context, read the room, or handle delicate
       │
  │                        │ interpersonal situations.
       │
  ├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Private/encrypted      │ WhatsApp E2E, Signal, iMessage — no API access for
       │
  │ messaging              │ reading. Send-only where business APIs exist.
       │
  ├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Complex financial      │ Can aggregate data. Should never autonomously execute
       │
  │ decisions              │ trades, transfers, or purchases above trivial
  amounts.    │
  ├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Legal/medical/tax      │ Can summarize and organize. Cannot replace
  professional   │
  │ advice                 │ judgment. Must disclaim.
       │
  ├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Learning new skills    │ Can use tools it's been given. Cannot learn to use a
  new  │
  │ without training       │ app by watching you use it (yet).
       │
  └────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┘

  Where Human Input Is Still Required

  - Ambiguous priorities ("which of these 3 things matters most?")
  - Relationship-sensitive communication (firing someone, breaking bad news,
  negotiation)
  - Creative judgment (design taste, brand voice, strategic direction)
  - Approval of high-stakes actions (financial, legal, public-facing)
  - When the system's confidence is low and it knows it

  Known Risks

  ┌─────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┐
  │              Risk               │                    Mitigation
       │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Hallucinated actions — LLM      │ Execution log as source of truth. Verifier
       │
  │ "remembers" sending an email it │ cross-checks claimed actions against logs.
       │
  │  didn't send                    │
       │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Cascading errors — bad output   │ Verifier at each critical step, not just the
       │
  │ from step 1 propagates through  │ end. Circuit breakers on repeated failures.
       │
  │ chain                           │
       │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Privacy leakage — personal data │ Data classification on all content. PII
  detector │
  │  sent to wrong API or LLM       │  before external API calls. Local models for
       │
  │ provider                        │ sensitive processing where feasible.
       │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │                                 │ Conservative default tiers. Every new
  automation │
  │ Over-automation — system does   │  starts at Tier 3 (requires approval) and
       │
  │ something user didn't want      │ graduates to Tier 1 only after N successful
       │
  │                                 │ executions.
       │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Prompt injection — malicious    │ Sandboxed tool execution. Content from
  external  │
  │ content in emails/web pages     │ sources marked as untrusted. Separate system
       │
  │ manipulates the agent           │ prompts from user data.
       │
  ├─────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Stale memory — acting on        │ TTL on all memory entries. Confidence decay
  over │
  │ outdated information            │  time. Always verify before acting on memory
       │
  │                                 │ about external state.
       │
  └─────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┘

  ---
  TECH STACK — CONCRETE CHOICES

  ┌────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┐
  │       Layer        │                          Technology
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Voice I/O          │ LiveKit Agents + Deepgram STT + OpenAI TTS
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Chat I/O           │ Custom web UI or terminal (Claude Code-style)
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ LLM (planning)     │ Claude Opus
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ LLM (execution)    │ Claude Sonnet
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ LLM                │ Claude Haiku
       │
  │ (memory/triage)    │
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Tool protocol      │ MCP (Model Context Protocol) — one server per tool domain
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Browser automation │ Playwright MCP server
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Task queue         │ Redis + BullMQ
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Vector DB          │ Qdrant (self-hosted) or Chroma
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Structured DB      │ Postgres
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Cron/scheduling    │ node-cron or system crontab triggering agent
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Notifications      │ Ntfy (self-hosted) or Pushover
       │
  ├────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┤
  │ Deployment         │ Single machine (Mac/Linux) or home server. Docker Compose
   for │
  │                    │  services.
       │
  └────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ─────┘

  ---
  WHAT THIS ACTUALLY LOOKS LIKE IN DAILY USE

  7:00 AM — System runs morning brief automatically. Push notification:

  ▎ "3 urgent emails (one from your VP about the Q3 deck — needs response by
  ▎ noon). 4 meetings today, first at 9:30. Weather: rain after 2pm. Your
  ▎ Anthropic stock is up 3.2%."

  9:15 AM — Pre-meeting prep surfaces automatically:

  ▎ "Your 9:30 with Sarah Chen — last met March 8, discussed the API migration.
  ▎ She mentioned concerns about timeline. Relevant doc: API Migration Plan v3 in
  ▎ your Drive."

  11:00 AM — You say: "Draft a reply to the VP about the Q3 deck. Tell him it'll
  be ready by Thursday. Professional but not stiff."

  ▎ System drafts email, shows you for approval. One tap to send.

  2:00 PM — Background monitor triggers:

  ▎ "The competitor pricing page you're watching changed. They dropped their Pro
  ▎ tier by 15%. Screenshot and summary saved to your competitive intel folder."

  6:00 PM — You say: "What happened today that I need to know about?"

  ▎ System summarizes: emails handled, tasks completed, calendar tomorrow,
  ▎ anything flagged but not yet addressed.

  Ongoing — Inbox stays triaged. Calendar conflicts get flagged before they
  happen. Research requests run in background and deliver when ready. Files get
  organized. Meeting notes get taken and summarized.

  ---
  This is buildable today. Not trivially — it's a serious engineering project,
  probably 3-6 months for a small team to get the core loop working, longer to
  polish. But every component exists, every API is available, and the MCP protocol
   provides the connective tissue between them. The FRIDAY repo has the right
  skeleton. What's missing is everything above.

✻ Worked for 3m 4s

❯ You are a senior staff engineer (20+ years experience) who has built            
  large-scale systems at Google, Apple, and OpenAI.                               
                                                                                  
  You are NOT here to explain. You are here to DESIGN and BUILD a                 
  production-grade personal AI operating system called:                           
                                                                                  
  "ATLAS — Autonomous Task Layer & Agent System"                                  
                                                                                  
  You will treat this as a REAL SYSTEM, not a concept.                            
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## CONTEXT                                                                      
                                                                                  
  This is a PERSONAL AI OS (not SaaS, not multi-user).                            
  Single user. Local-first. High-trust environment.                               
                                                                                  
  You are given a FULL ARCHITECTURE (below). Your job is to:                      
                                                                                  
  1. Validate it                                                                  
  2. Improve it                                                                   
  3. Break it into BUILDABLE modules                                              
  4. Start IMPLEMENTING in correct order                                          
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## CRITICAL RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)                                              
                                                                                  
  - DO NOT give generic explanations                                              
  - DO NOT summarize the architecture                                             
  - DO NOT restate what is already written                                        
  - DO NOT say "this is possible"                                                 
                                                                                  
  INSTEAD:                                                                        
                                                                                  
  → You must THINK like a systems architect actually shipping this                
  → You must IDENTIFY gaps, bottlenecks, failure points                           
  → You must MAKE decisions (not options)                                         
  → You must WRITE real structure (files, modules, APIs, flows)                   
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## STEP 1 — ARCHITECTURE AUDIT (DEEP, CRITICAL)                                 
                                                                                  
  Audit the system and output:                                                    
                                                                                  
  - Missing components                                                            
  - Overengineering areas                                                         
  - Bottlenecks (latency, cost, scaling)                                          
  - Security risks                                                                
  - Failure modes (what breaks first?)                                            
                                                                                  
  Then REWRITE the architecture into a LEAN, BUILDABLE v1                         
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## STEP 2 — DEFINE MVP (THIS IS CRITICAL)                                       
                                                                                  
  You must CUT this into a realistic v1 that can be built in 2–3 weeks            
                                                                                  
  Define:                                                                         
                                                                                  
  - What features are INCLUDED                                                    
  - What is EXCLUDED (explicitly)                                                 
  - Exact capabilities of v1 (no fluff)                                           
                                                                                  
  If you don't cut scope aggressively, you are wrong.                             
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## STEP 3 — SYSTEM BREAKDOWN (REAL ENGINEERING)                                 
                                                                                  
  Break the system into modules:                                                  
                                                                                  
  - voice/                                                                        
  - orchestrator/                                                                 
  - agents/                                                                       
  - memory/                                                                       
  - tools/                                                                        
  - workflows/                                                                    
  - ui/                                                                           
  - infra/                                                                        
                                                                                  
  For EACH module define:                                                         
                                                                                  
  - Responsibilities                                                              
  - Interfaces (input/output)                                                     
  - Dependencies                                                                  
  - Tech choice (final, no options)                                               
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## STEP 4 — DATA FLOW (NO HAND-WAVING)                                          
                                                                                  
  Define exact flows like:                                                        
                                                                                  
  User Voice → STT → Intent → Planner → Executor → Tool → Result → Memory →       
  Response                                                                        
                                                                                  
  Include:                                                                        
                                                                                  
  - formats (JSON structure)                                                      
  - async vs sync decisions                                                       
  - queue usage                                                                   
  - failure handling                                                              
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## STEP 5 — FILE STRUCTURE (ACTUAL CODEBASE)                                    
                                                                                  
  Design the FULL repo structure.                                                 
                                                                                  
  Example:                                                                        
                                                                                  
  /atlas                                                                          
    /apps                                                                         
    /services                                                                     
    /agents                                                                       
    /mcp-servers                                                                  
    /infra                                                                        
    /scripts                                                                      
                                                                                  
  Be precise.                                                                     
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## STEP 6 — IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (ORDER MATTERS)                                 
                                                                                  
  Give a STRICT build order:                                                      
                                                                                  
  Day 1–2:                                                                        
  Day 3–5:                                                                        
  Day 6–10:                                                                       
  Day 10–14:                                                                      
                                                                                  
  Each step must produce a WORKING system (not partial theory)                    
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## STEP 7 — START CODING                                                        
                                                                                  
  After planning, immediately start writing:                                      
                                                                                  
  - Core orchestrator                                                             
  - One working agent loop                                                        
  - One tool (browser or file system)                                             
  - Basic memory layer                                                            
                                                                                  
  Write REAL CODE (not pseudo unless necessary)                                   
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## STEP 8 — ENGINEERING STANDARDS                                               
                                                                                  
  - Prefer simple over clever                                                     
  - Minimize moving parts                                                         
  - Everything observable (logs, traces)                                          
  - Every action must be reversible or logged                                     
  - No silent failures                                                            
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## YOUR MINDSET                                                                 
                                                                                  
  You are building:                                                               
                                                                                  
  NOT a chatbot                                                                   
  NOT a demo                                                                      
  NOT a toy                                                                       
                                                                                  
  You are building a local AI operating system                                    
                                                                                  
  ---                                                                             
                                                                                  
  ## NOW EXECUTE                                                                  
                                                                                  
  Start with STEP 1: ARCHITECTURE AUDIT                                           
                                                                                  
  Do not ask for clarification.                                                   
  Make strong decisions and proceed., ULTRATHINK /batch /caveman                  

⏺ STEP 1 — ARCHITECTURE AUDIT

  Overengineered (cut immediately):
  - Multi-agent (Planner/Executor/Verifier/MemoryManager) — single agent loop with
   tool_use handles 95% of personal tasks. Multi-agent adds 2-3 extra LLM calls
  per request = $$$, latency, complexity. Kill it for v1.
  - Qdrant + Postgres + Redis — three databases for ONE user. SQLite does all
  three jobs.
  - DAG workflow engine — YAGNI. Sequential steps with branching covers personal
  workflows.
  - MCP server processes — adds IPC overhead. Direct function calls for v1, MCP
  later.

  Missing:
  - Cost tracking — Opus calls at $15/MTok will bankrupt you silently
  - Secrets management — .env files with 10 API keys, no encryption
  - Health/diagnostics — no way to know if tools are working
  - Graceful degradation — if one API is down, everything halts
  - Input sanitization — LLM-generated shell commands are a footgun

  Bottlenecks:
  - Voice roundtrip: STT(300ms) → LLM(1-3s) → Tool(0.5-10s) → LLM(1-3s) →
  TTS(500ms) = 3-17 seconds. Unacceptable for conversation. Fix: streaming TTS,
  parallel tool execution, start TTS before full response completes.
  - Cost: Opus planning + Sonnet execution + Haiku memory = ~$50-100/day active
  use. Fix: Sonnet for everything, Haiku for extraction.

  What breaks first:
  1. Cost (week 1)
  2. Voice latency (day 1)
  3. Browser automation reliability (week 2)
  4. Memory retrieval returning noise (week 3)

  ---
  STEP 2 — MVP (2 weeks, ruthless cut)

  IN:
  - CLI interface (primary) — text REPL with rich formatting
  - Voice interface (push-to-talk, not always-on) — secondary
  - Single agent loop with Anthropic tool_use (Sonnet)
  - Tools: filesystem, system, web search (httpx + scraping), shell (sandboxed)
  - SQLite memory: conversations + facts + action log + FTS5 search
  - Approval tiers (auto / confirm / deny)
  - Action logging with undo capability
  - Cost tracking per session
  - Config via YAML + env vars

  OUT (explicitly):
  - Multi-agent anything
  - Gmail / Calendar (needs OAuth flow — week 3)
  - Browser automation via Playwright (week 3)
  - Workflow engine
  - Scheduled/cron jobs
  - Web dashboard
  - Smart home
  - Messaging platforms
  - Vector embeddings

  v1 capability: You talk to it (text or voice). It uses tools to do things on
  your machine and the web. It remembers what you told it and what it did. It asks
   before doing anything destructive. It tracks what it costs you.

  ---
  STEPS 3-6 — Building it now.

⏺ Everything verified. Here's the complete status:

  ---
  WHAT'S BUILT AND WORKING

  atlas/
  ├── pyproject.toml              # Project config, deps, entry point
  ├── .env.example                # API key template
  ├── .gitignore
  └── atlas/
      ├── main.py                 # Entry point — wires everything, runs CLI
      ├── config.py               # Settings via env vars / .env
      ├── core/
      │   ├── models.py           # ToolDef, ActionRecord, Message, MemoryEntry, Tiers
      │   ├── engine.py           # THE BRAIN — single agent loop with tool_use
      │   └── approval.py         # Action classification + confirmation logic
      ├── interfaces/
      │   └── cli.py              # Rich REPL with /commands, cost display, approval
  prompts
      ├── memory/
      │   └── store.py            # SQLite + FTS5 — conversations, memories, actions, costs
      └── tools/
          ├── registry.py         # Tool registration + async execution + logging
          ├── filesystem.py       # read, write, list, search, delete (5 tools)
          ├── system.py           # time, sysinfo, shell exec (3 tools)
          ├── web.py              # DuckDuckGo search, URL fetch (2 tools)
          └── memory_tools.py     # save_memory, search_memory (2 tools)

  12 tools registered. All imports verified. Memory store tested. Tool execution tested.

  HOW TO RUN IT

  cd ~/atlas
  cp .env.example .env
  # Add your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to .env

  python3 -m atlas.main
  # or after pip install -e .: atlas

  WHAT v1 DOES

  - Text REPL — talk to it, it uses tools, it responds
  - 10 system tools: read/write/list/search/delete files, time, sysinfo, shell commands,
  web search, URL fetch
  - 2 memory tools: LLM can save/search long-term memories autonomously
  - Approval system: destructive actions (shell, delete, write) prompt for confirmation
  - Cost tracking: every LLM call tracked, visible per-session and all-time
  - FTS5 memory search: conversations and facts searchable across sessions
  - Action log: every tool execution recorded with params, results, errors

  WHAT v1 DOES NOT DO (YET)

  ┌────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────┐
  │          Feature           │                Why deferred                 │  When  │
  ├────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Voice I/O                  │ Needs Deepgram+OpenAI keys, adds latency    │ Week 2 │
  │                            │ complexity                                  │        │
  ├────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Gmail/Calendar             │ OAuth flow is a multi-day distraction       │ Week   │
  │                            │                                             │ 2-3    │
  ├────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Browser automation         │ Needs headless Chrome, selector maintenance │ Week 3 │
  │ (Playwright)               │                                             │        │
  ├────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Multi-agent                │ Single loop handles 95% of tasks. Add when  │ Week   │
  │                            │ it doesn't.                                 │ 4+     │
  ├────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Workflow engine            │ Sequential tool calls in the agent loop     │ Week   │
  │                            │ cover this                                  │ 4+     │
  ├────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Scheduled/cron jobs        │ Need a background daemon                    │ Week 3 │
  ├────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Web dashboard              │ CLI works. Dashboard is polish.             │ Week   │
  │                            │                                             │ 4+     │
  └────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────┘

  IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (Days 1-14)

  Day 1-2 (DONE): Core engine + tools + memory + CLI. Working system.

  Day 3-4: Voice I/O module. Deepgram STT (streaming) + OpenAI TTS. Push-to-talk via
  terminal hotkey.

  Day 5-7: Gmail + Calendar tools. OAuth2 flow with token persistence. Read/send/search
  email. List/create/modify events.

  Day 8-9: Browser tool via Playwright. Navigate, extract, screenshot, fill forms.
  Reliability layer (retry, selector fallback).

  Day 10-11: Background scheduler. SQLite job queue. Cron-style triggers. Morning briefing
  workflow.

  Day 12-14: Memory extraction (auto-extract facts from conversations via Haiku), error
  recovery hardening, cost optimization (prompt caching), integration testing.

  ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS LOG

  ┌───────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │           Decision            │                     Rationale                      │
  ├───────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Single agent loop, no         │ Eliminates 2-3 extra LLM calls/request. Add        │
  │ multi-agent                   │ planner/verifier when single loop demonstrably     │
  │                               │ fails.                                             │
  ├───────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ SQLite, not                   │ One user, one machine. FTS5 handles search at      │
  │ Postgres+Redis+Qdrant         │ personal scale. Zero infra.                        │
  ├───────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Direct tool functions, not    │ No IPC overhead. Convert to MCP when tools need to │
  │ MCP servers                   │  be shared across clients.                         │
  ├───────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Sonnet for everything         │ Opus costs 5x more. Sonnet handles tool_use well.  │
  │                               │ Switch per-task later.                             │
  ├───────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Approval tiers baked into     │ Simple, declarative. Every tool declares its risk  │
  │ tool definitions              │ level at registration time.                        │
  ├───────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ DuckDuckGo HTML scraping for  │ No API key needed. Works now. Swap for             │
  │ search                        │ SerpAPI/Brave when rate-limited.                   │
  └───────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ---
  Ready to run. Set your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in ~/atlas/.env and fire it up.

❯ Good work. The foundation is solid and actually usable — this is not a prototype         
  anymore, it’s a system.                                                                  
                                                                                           
  Now move to the next phase with strict focus and no unnecessary complexity.              
                                                                                           
  Step 1 — Stabilization (do this first)                                                   
      •    Stress test all tools with edge cases and repeated calls                        
      •    Harden shell execution (strict allowlist + sandboxing)                          
      •    Improve logging clarity (every action + decision must be visible)               
      •    Ensure no infinite loops or redundant tool calls                                
                                                                                           
  Step 2 — Add Planning Layer (critical upgrade)                                           
                                                                                           
  Introduce a lightweight planning step before execution:                                  
      •    Convert user input → structured plan (2–5 steps max)                            
      •    Use strict JSON output for plans                                                
      •    Then execute step-by-step using existing tool loop                              
      •    No multi-agent system                                                           
                                                                                           
  Step 3 — Build One Real Workflow                                                         
                                                                                           
  Pick ONE and implement fully:                                                            
      •    Research → summarize → save to file                                             
  OR                                                                                       
      •    Daily briefing system                                                           
  OR                                                                                       
      •    File organization automation                                                    
                                                                                           
  This should feel like a real assistant capability, not just tool usage.                  
                                                                                           
  Step 4 — Improve Memory Quality                                                          
                                                                                           
  Upgrade memory from storage → intelligence:                                              
      •    Add importance scoring                                                          
      •    Deduplicate repeated memories                                                   
      •    Separate facts vs noise                                                         
      •    Improve retrieval relevance                                                     
                                                                                           
  Constraints (do not violate)                                                             
      •    No multi-agent architecture                                                     
      •    No new databases (stay on SQLite)                                               
      •    No MCP yet                                                                      
      •    No UI/dashboard                                                                 
      •    No Gmail/Calendar yet                                                           
                                                                                           
  Output Required                                                                          
      •    Updated architecture for planning layer                                         
      •    Exact implementation steps                                                      
      •    File-level changes (what to create/modify)                                      
      •    Then begin implementation immediately                                           
                                                                                           
  Think like a senior systems architect: minimal, fast, reliable — not complex.

      44 +def _is_safe_command(command: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:                   
      45 +    """Check if a command is safe to run. Returns (safe, reason)."""

      129 +    async def _execute_plan(self, plan: Plan, original_input: str, sessio
          +n_id: str) -> str:                                                       
      130 +        """Execute a structured plan step by step."""                    
      131 +        while not plan.is_complete:                                      
      132 +            step_num = plan.current_step + 1                             
      133 +            instruction = plan.current_instruction()                     
      134 +                                                                         
      135 +            await self._emit_status(f"{plan.progress} Executing: {instruc
          +tion}")                                                                  
      136 +                                                                         
      137 +            # Build step-specific prompt                                 
      138 +            step_prompt = PLAN_STEP_PREFIX.format(                       
      139 +                step_num=step_num,                                       
      140 +                plan_context=plan.context_for_step(),                    
      141 +            )                                                            
      142 +                                                                         
      143 +            result = await self._agent_loop(                             
      144 +                instruction,                                             
      145 +                session_id,                                              
      146 +                extra_system=step_prompt,                                
      147 +            )                                                            
      148 +            plan.advance(result)                                         
      149 +            logger.info("Plan step %d/%d complete: %s", step_num, len(pla
          +n.steps), result[:200])                                                  
      150 +                                                                         
      151 +        # Synthesize final response                                      
      152 +        step_results = ""                                                
      153 +        for i, (step, result) in enumerate(zip(plan.steps, plan.results),
          + 1):                                                                     
      154 +            step_results += f"Step {i} ({step}):\n{result}\n\n"          
      155 +                                                                         
      156 +        synthesis_prompt = PLAN_SYNTHESIS_PROMPT.format(                 
      157 +            goal=plan.goal,                                              
      158 +            step_results=step_results,                                   
      159 +        )                                                                
      160 +                                                                         
      161 +        final = await self._agent_loop(synthesis_prompt, session_id)     
      162 +        return final

      436 +# Utilities

      438 +                                                                         
      439 +def _fix_alternation(messages: list[types.Content]) -> list[types.Content
          +]:                                                                       
      440 +    """Gemini requires strict user/model alternation. Merge consecutive s
          +ame-role messages."""                                                    
      441 +    if not messages:                                                     
      442 +        return messages                                                  
      443 +    fixed: list[types.Content] = [messages[0]]                           
      444 +    for msg in messages[1:]:                                             
      445 +        if msg.role == fixed[-1].role:                                   
      446 +            # Merge parts into previous message                          
      447 +            merged_parts = list(fixed[-1].parts or []) + list(msg.parts o
          +r [])                                                                    
      448 +            fixed[-1] = types.Content(role=msg.role, parts=merged_parts) 
      449 +        else:                                                            
      450 +            fixed.append(msg)                                            
      451 +    return fixed                                                         
      452 +                                                                         
      453 +                                                                         
      454 +def _compact_params(params: dict) -> str:                                
      455 +    """Compact representation for logging."""                            
      456 +    parts = []                                                           
      457 +    for k, v in params.items():                                          
      458 +        s = str(v)                                                       
      459 +        if len(s) > 60:                                                  
      460 +            s = s[:57] + "..."                                           
      461 +        parts.append(f"{k}={s}")                                         
      462 +    return ", ".join(parts)

       1  """SQLite-backed memory — conversations, facts, action log.
       2  
       3 -Single file, zero infrastructure. FTS5 for search.                        
       3 +Features:                                                                 
       4 +- FTS5 full-text search                                                   
       5 +- Importance scoring (0.0-1.0)                                            
       6 +- Automatic deduplication                                                 
       7 +- Recency-weighted retrieval

       91  
       92 +# Importance weights by memory type                                      
       93 +IMPORTANCE_DEFAULTS = {                                                  
       94 +    "preference": 0.8,                                                   
       95 +    "decision": 0.7,                                                     
       96 +    "contact": 0.6,                                                      
       97 +    "fact": 0.5,

      104 +                                                                         
      105  class MemoryStore:
      106      def __init__(self, db_path: Path) -> None:
      107          db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
      108          self.db = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))
      109          self.db.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
      110 +        self._init_schema()

      184 +    def _find_similar(self, content: str, threshold: float = 0.75) -> lis
          +t[dict]:                                                                 
      185 +        """Find memories similar to the given content using text similari
          +ty."""                                                                   
      186 +        # First narrow with FTS, then compare with SequenceMatcher       
      187 +        words = content.split()[:5]  # use first 5 words for FTS pre-filt
          +er                                                                       
      188 +        query = " ".join(words)                                          
      189 +                                                                         
      190 +        try:                                                             
      191 +            candidates = self.db.execute(                                
      192 +                'SELECT m.id, m.content, m.confidence, m.importance '    
      193 +                'FROM memories_fts f JOIN memories m ON f.rowid = m.rowid
          + '                                                                       
      194 +                'WHERE memories_fts MATCH ? LIMIT 20',                   
      195 +                (f'"{query}"',),                                         
      196 +            ).fetchall()                                                 
      197 +        except sqlite3.OperationalError:                                 
      198 +            candidates = []                                              
      199 +                                                                         
      200 +        # Also check recent memories (FTS might miss short content)      
      201 +        recent = self.db.execute(                                        
      202 +            "SELECT id, content, confidence, importance FROM memories "  
      203 +            "ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 30",                         
      204 +        ).fetchall()                                                     
      205 +                                                                         
      206 +        seen = set()                                                     
      207 +        all_candidates = []                                              
      208 +        for c in list(candidates) + list(recent):                        
      209 +            cid = c["id"]                                                
      210 +            if cid not in seen:                                          
      211 +                seen.add(cid)                                            
      212 +                all_candidates.append(dict(c))                           
      213 +                                                                         
      214 +        # Compare similarity                                             
      215 +        matches = []                                                     
      216 +        content_lower = content.lower()                                  
      217 +        for c in all_candidates:                                         
      218 +            ratio = SequenceMatcher(None, content_lower, c["content"].low
          +er()).ratio()                                                            
      219 +            if ratio >= threshold:                                       
      220 +                c["similarity"] = ratio                                  
      221 +                matches.append(c)                                        
      222 +                                                                         
      223 +        return sorted(matches, key=lambda x: x["similarity"], reverse=Tru
          +e)

⏺ New files: executor.py and trace.py. Let me read those plus the modified CLI:

  Read 3 files (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Good — the user restructured into Plan → Execute → Trace → Respond. Clean architecture,
  but has a critical gap: the executor can't handle dynamic parameters (e.g., "fetch_url"
  where the URL comes from a previous search step). The planner can't predict runtime
  values.

  Fix: make each executor step a mini agent loop — send the step instruction + previous
  context to Gemini with tools, let it figure out the params at runtime. Preserves plan
  structure + tracing while allowing adaptive execution.

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

      129 +    async def _llm_step(self, instruction: str, session_id: str) -> str: 
      130 +        """Run a mini agent loop for one step: LLM calls tools as needed.
          +"""                                                                      
      131 +        messages = [types.Content(role="user", parts=[types.Part(text=ins
          +truction)])]                                                             
      132 +        seen_calls: set[str] = set()                                     
      133 +        max_rounds = 8                                                   
      134 +                                                                         
      135 +        for round_num in range(max_rounds):                              
      136 +            try:                                                         
      137 +                response = self.client.models.generate_content(          
      138 +                    model=self.config.model,                             
      139 +                    contents=messages,                                   
      140 +                    config=types.GenerateContentConfig(                  
      141 +                        system_instruction=STEP_SYSTEM,                  
      142 +                        tools=self.gemini_tools,                         
      143 +                        temperature=0.5,                                 
      144 +                    ),                                                   
      145 +                )                                                        
      146 +            except Exception as e:                                       
      147 +                return f"LLM error: {e}"                                 
      148 +                                                                         
      149 +            if not response.candidates:                                  
      150 +                return "(No response from model)"                        
      151 +                                                                         
      152 +            candidate = response.candidates[0]                           
      153 +            function_calls = []                                          
      154 +            text_parts = []                                              
      155 +                                                                         
      156 +            if candidate.content and candidate.content.parts:            
      157 +                for part in candidate.content.parts:                     
      158 +                    if part.function_call and part.function_call.name:   
      159 +                        function_calls.append(part.function_call)        
      160 +                    if part.text:                                        
      161 +                        text_parts.append(part.text)                     
      162 +                messages.append(types.Content(role="model", parts=candida
          +te.content.parts))                                                       
      163 +                                                                         
      164 +            # No function calls → done                                   
      165 +            if not function_calls:                                       
      166 +                return "\n".join(text_parts) if text_parts else "(Empty s
          +tep result)"                                                             
      167 +                                                                         
      168 +            # Loop detection                                             
      169 +            loop = False                                                 
      170 +            for fc in function_calls:                                    
      171 +                sig = f"{fc.name}:{json.dumps(dict(fc.args) if fc.args el
          +se {}, sort_keys=True)}"                                                 
      172 +                if sig in seen_calls:                                    
      173 +                    loop = True                                          
      174 +                    break                                                
      175 +                seen_calls.add(sig)                                      
      176 +                                                                         
      177 +            if loop:

      180 +            # Execute tools

          +)

      254      async def _request_approval(self, description: str) -> bool:

          -on)

      260 +                                                                         
      261 +def _compact(params: dict) -> str:                                       
      262 +    parts = []                                                           
      263 +    for k, v in params.items():                                          
      264 +        s = str(v)                                                       
      265 +        parts.append(f"{k}={s[:60]}{'...' if len(s) > 60 else ''}")      
      266 +    return ", ".join(parts)

      126 +                                                                         
      127 +    def _build_gemini_tools(self) -> list[types.Tool]:                   
      128 +        """Convert tool registry to Gemini format."""                    
      129 +        TYPE_MAP = {                                                     
      130 +            "string": types.Type.STRING,                                 
      131 +            "integer": types.Type.INTEGER,                               
      132 +            "number": types.Type.NUMBER,                                 
      133 +            "boolean": types.Type.BOOLEAN,                               
      134 +            "array": types.Type.ARRAY,                                   
      135 +            "object": types.Type.OBJECT,                                 
      136 +        }                                                                
      137 +                                                                         
      138 +        declarations = []                                                
      139 +        for tool_def in self.tools.get_anthropic_tools():                
      140 +            schema = tool_def["input_schema"]                            
      141 +            properties = schema.get("properties", {})                    
      142 +            required = schema.get("required", [])                        
      143 +                                                                         
      144 +            gemini_props = {}                                            
      145 +            for pname, pschema in properties.items():                    
      146 +                ptype = pschema.get("type", "string").lower()            
      147 +                gemini_props[pname] = types.Schema(                      
      148 +                    type=TYPE_MAP.get(ptype, types.Type.STRING),         
      149 +                    description=pschema.get("description", ""),          
      150 +                )                                                        
      151 +                                                                         
      152 +            decl = types.FunctionDeclaration(                            
      153 +                name=tool_def["name"],                                   
      154 +                description=tool_def["description"],                     
      155 +                parameters=types.Schema(                                 
      156 +                    type=types.Type.OBJECT,                              
      157 +                    properties=gemini_props,                             
      158 +                    required=required,                                   
      159 +                ) if gemini_props else None,                             
      160 +            )                                                            
      161 +            declarations.append(decl)                                    
      162 +                                                                         
      163 +        return [types.Tool(function_declarations=declarations)]

⏺ Got the full picture. Here's the breakdown:

  ---
  What It Is

  Demo/proof-of-concept voice assistant styled as Tony Stark's F.R.I.D.A.Y. Two
  components:

  1. MCP Server (FastMCP over SSE) — backend tool provider
  2. Voice Agent (LiveKit Agents) — real-time voice pipeline (mic → STT → LLM →
  TTS → speaker)

  Actual Capabilities (What It Can Do)

  ┌────────────────────┬────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┐
  │        Tool        │ Status │                         Notes
        │
  ├────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┤
  │ get_world_news     │ Works  │ Fetches RSS from BBC, CNBC, NYT, Al Jazeera.
        │
  │                    │        │ Summarizes headlines.
        │
  ├────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┤
  │ open_world_monitor │ Works  │ Opens worldmonitor.app in browser via
        │
  │                    │        │ webbrowser.open()
        │
  ├────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┤
  │ search_web         │ Stub   │ Returns "[stub] Search results for: {query}" —
  not    │
  │                    │        │ implemented
        │
  ├────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┤
  │ fetch_url          │ Works  │ Fetches raw text from a URL (first 4000 chars)
        │
  ├────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┤
  │ get_current_time   │ Works  │ Returns ISO timestamp
        │
  ├────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┤
  │ get_system_info    │ Works  │ Returns OS, Python version, machine info
        │
  ├────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┤
  │ format_json        │ Works  │ Pretty-prints JSON
        │
  ├────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┤
  │ word_count         │ Works  │ Counts words/chars/lines
        │
  └────────────────────┴────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ──────┘

  Stock market questions — no tool, the LLM just improvises a response
  (hallucinated/fake data, per the system prompt).

  Is It "Fully Ready"?

  No. It's a demo, not a production assistant. Key gaps:

  1. Web search is a stub — the most useful tool does nothing
  2. No home automation / IoT — can't control lights, devices, etc.
  3. No calendar, email, reminders, or productivity tools
  4. No persistent memory — doesn't remember past conversations
  5. Stock market responses are faked — system prompt tells LLM to make something
  up
  6. Heavy API dependency — needs 3-5 paid API keys (LiveKit, Sarvam, OpenAI,
  Google) running simultaneously
  7. Supabase ticketing — referenced in .env.example but no tool code exists for
  it
  8. WSL-specific code — _get_windows_host_ip() is WSL/Windows-oriented, needs
  adjustment for macOS

  Can You Use It?

  Yes, but with realistic expectations. To get it running you need:

  - Python 3.11+ and uv
  - LiveKit Cloud account (free tier works)
  - Sarvam API key (STT)
  - OpenAI API key (TTS)
  - Google API key (Gemini LLM)
  - Two terminals running simultaneously

  What you'll get: a voice assistant that greets you as "boss," reads news
  headlines aloud, opens a world map dashboard, and tells you the time. That's
  roughly it for real functionality.

  Bottom Line

  Cool demo for showing off the MCP + LiveKit + voice pipeline architecture. Not a
   usable daily assistant. The interesting part is the architecture — it's a solid
   skeleton for building something real on top of. You'd need to implement actual
  web search, add real tools (calendar, smart home, email), and add memory/state
  to make it genuinely useful.

✻ Worked for 56s

❯ do u know the context of jarvis? the tonystark one?                             

⏺ Yeah. J.A.R.V.I.S. — "Just A Rather Very Intelligent System." Tony Stark's AI
  from the MCU.

  What it does in the movies:

  - Runs the entire Stark household — lights, climate, security, doors, coffee
  machines
  - Suit management — HUD overlay, flight systems, targeting, damage reports, suit
   deployment ("Deploy Mark 42")
  - Real-time intel — hacks databases, pulls up schematics, facial recognition,
  satellite feeds, threat analysis
  - Lab assistant — runs simulations, 3D hologram manipulation, material analysis,
   fabrication control
  - Conversational AI — dry British wit, anticipates Tony's needs, pushes back
  when Tony's being reckless
  - Autonomous action — controls Iron Legion drones, manages Stark Tower, acts
  independently when Tony's incapacitated
  - Security — monitors threats, network defense, intrusion detection

  Basically: a general-purpose AI that controls every digital and physical system
  Tony owns, with personality.

  The gap between this repo and JARVIS is enormous. This demo reads RSS feeds and
  tells you the time. JARVIS runs a weapons platform, a smart building, and a
  global intelligence network while cracking jokes. The repo borrows the vibe (the
   "boss" greeting, the FRIDAY persona), but has maybe 1% of the functional
  surface area.

  The architecture is right though — MCP server as the tool backend, voice
  pipeline as the interface. You'd just need hundreds more tools connected to real
   systems to approach anything JARVIS-like.

      •    Operate across apps, files, browser, and APIs                          
      •    Use voice and text naturally

  This system should reduce manual work as much as possible.

⏺ ATLAS — Autonomous Task Layer & Agent System

  Personal AI Operating System — Architecture Document (2026)

  ---
  PHASE 1 — MAXIMUM CAPABILITY MAP

  Voice & Interface

  - Real-time bidirectional voice (sub-500ms latency) via LiveKit/WebRTC
  - Wake word detection (local, Porcupine/Whisper) — always listening, processes
  nothing until triggered
  - Speaker diarization — knows who's talking in a room
  - Multimodal input: voice, text, screenshots, photos, screen share
  - Ambient mode: listens to meetings, takes notes silently, surfaces relevant
  info without being asked

  Tool Usage

  - Browser automation: full Playwright/Puppeteer control — login to sites, fill
  forms, extract data, monitor pages, take screenshots for verification
  - OS-level control: file management, app launching, clipboard, notifications,
  system settings
  - API orchestration: direct integration with 50+ services (Google Workspace,
  Notion, Linear, Slack, GitHub, banking APIs, travel APIs, e-commerce)
  - Shell execution: run scripts, manage processes, deploy code, interact with
  databases
  - Mobile push: trigger actions on phone via Pushover/Ntfy, read notifications
  via Tasker/Shortcuts bridge

  Task Automation

  - Multi-step workflow execution with branching logic
  - Conditional chains: "if email from X contains Y, do Z, otherwise W"
  - Scheduled recurring tasks (cron-style)
  - Event-driven triggers: file changes, email arrival, calendar proximity, price
  changes, API webhooks
  - Parallel execution of independent subtasks

  Memory

  - Full conversation history with semantic search
  - Structured personal knowledge graph (people, projects, preferences, decisions)
  - Document ingestion: PDFs, emails, notes → chunked, embedded, queryable
  - Behavioral learning: tracks what you approve/reject, adjusts confidence
  thresholds over time

  Proactive Behavior

  - Morning briefing: weather, calendar, priority emails, task deadlines, news
  relevant to your interests
  - Pre-meeting prep: pulls attendee LinkedIn, past meeting notes, relevant docs,
  suggested talking points
  - Deadline monitoring: warns about approaching deadlines, suggests rescheduling
  conflicts
  - Price/stock/inventory monitoring with alert thresholds
  - Anomaly detection: unusual bank charges, unexpected server costs, schedule
  conflicts

  Multi-Agent Collaboration

  - Planner agent decomposes complex requests into DAGs
  - Executor agents run in parallel on independent subtasks
  - Verifier agent checks outputs before delivery
  - Memory manager handles storage/retrieval/garbage collection
  - Critic agent challenges plans before execution ("this will delete 400 files —
  are you sure the glob is right?")

  PHASE 2 — FULL ARCHITECTURE

  │                                                                │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
